item.title: HackerRank Problem 1
item.description:
Table of Contents

Problem: Say "Hello, World!" With C++

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int main() {
    cout << "Hello, World!\n";

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

Hello, World!

item.title: HackerRank Problem 2
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: Input and Output

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int a, b, c;
int sum;

void additionOperator() {
    cin >> a >> b >> c;

    sum = a + b + c;
}

int main() {
   additionOperator();
   cout << sum << endl;

   return 0;
}

Demonstration

1 2 7
10

item.title: HackerRank Problem 3
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: Basic Data Types

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
#include <iomanip>
using namespace std;

int a;
long int b;
char c;
float d;
double e;

void getInput() {
    cin >> a >> b >> c >> d >> e;
}

int main() {
    getInput();
    cout << a << endl
         << setprecision(10000) << b << endl
         << c << endl
         << d << endl
         << setprecision(10000) << e << endl;

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

3 12345678912345 a 334.23 14049.30493
3
12345678912345
a
334.230010986328125
14049.304930000000240397639572620391845703125

item.title: HackerRank Problem 4
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: Conditional Statements

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int input;

int main() {
    cin >> input;

    if (input == 1) {
        cout << "one";
    } else if (input == 2) {
        cout << "two";
    } else if (input == 3) {
        cout << "three";
    } else if (input == 4) {
        cout << "four";
    } else if (input == 5) {
        cout << "five";
    } else if (input == 6) {
        cout << "six";
    } else if (input == 7) {
        cout << "seven";
    } else if (input == 8) {
        cout << "eight";
    } else if (input == 9) {
        cout << "nine";
    } else {
        cout << "Greater than 9";
    }

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

8
eight

44
Greater than 9

item.title: HackerRank Problem 5
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: For Loop

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int a;
int b;
int n;

int main() {
    cin >> a >> b;

    for (int i = a; i <= b; i++) {
        if (i == 1) {
            cout << "one" << endl;
        } else if (i == 2) {
            cout << "two" << endl;
        } else if (i == 3) {
            cout << "three" << endl;
        } else if (i == 4) {
            cout << "four" << endl;
        } else if (i == 5) {
            cout << "five" << endl;
        } else if (i == 6) {
            cout << "six" << endl;
        } else if (i == 7) {
            cout << "seven" << endl;
        } else if (i == 8) {
            cout << "eight" << endl;
        } else if (i == 9) {
            cout << "nine" << endl;
        } else if (i % 2 == 0) {
            cout << "even" << endl;
        } else {
            cout << "odd" << endl;
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

8
11
eight
nine
even
odd

item.title: HackerRank Problem 6
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: Functions

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int a;
int b;
int c;
int d;
int maxValue = 0;

void findMax() {
    cin >> a >> b >> c >> d;

    if (a > maxValue) {
        maxValue = a;
    } else {
        maxValue = maxValue;
    }

    if (b > maxValue) {
        maxValue = b;
    } else {
        maxValue = maxValue;
    }

    if (c > maxValue) {
        maxValue = c;
    } else {
        maxValue = maxValue;
    }

    if (d > maxValue) {
        maxValue = d;
    } else {
        maxValue = maxValue;
    }
}

int main() {
    findMax();
    cout << maxValue << endl;

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

3
4
6
5
6

item.title: HackerRank Problem 7
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: Pointer

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int a;
int b;
int sum;
int difference;

void addSubtract() {
    cin >> a >> b;

    sum = a + b;
    
    if (a >= b) {
        difference = a - b;
    } else {
        difference = b - a;
    }
}

int main() {
    addSubtract();
    cout << sum << endl;
    cout << difference << endl;

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

4
5
9
1

item.title: HackerRank Problem 8
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: Arrays Introduction

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int length;

int main() {
    cin >> length;

    int inputArray[length];
    int outputArray[length];

    for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {
        cin >> inputArray[i];
    }

    for (int j = 0; j < length; j++) {
        outputArray[j] = inputArray[length - j - 1];
    }


    for (int k = 0; k < length; k++) {
        cout << outputArray[k] << " ";
    }
    cout << endl;

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

4
1 4 3 2
2 3 4 1

item.title: HackerRank Problem 9
item.description:
Table of Contents
  • Problem
  • Source Code
  • Demonstration

Problem: Variable Sized Arrays

Link to the Challenge:

Problem Statement

Source Code

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
using namespace std;

int n;
int q;
int k;

vector<int> inputArray;
int userInput; 
int countInput = 0;
vector<vector<int>> collectArray;
vector<int> collectAnswer;

int arrNum;
int elemNum;
int elemValue;


void inputValues() {
    cin >> n >> q;

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        cin >> k;

        while (countInput < k && cin >> userInput) {
            inputArray.push_back(userInput);
            countInput++;
        }

        collectArray.push_back(inputArray);
        inputArray.clear();
        countInput = 0;
    }

    for (int j = 0; j < q; j++) {
        cin >> arrNum >> elemNum;

        elemValue = collectArray[arrNum][elemNum];
        
        collectAnswer.push_back(elemValue);
    }
}

int main() {
    inputValues();

    for (int ans = 0; ans < q; ans++) {
        cout << collectAnswer[ans] << endl;
    }

    return 0;
}

Demonstration

2 2
3 1 5 4
5 1 2 8 9 3
0 1
1 3
5
9
